content.full_text:
City of Santa Cruz
• CITY ATTORNEY •
333 CHURCH STREET
SANTA CRUZ, CALIFORNIA 95060
TELEPHONE (408) 429-3553

December 24, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Conflict of Interest Advice Request - Councilmember
     Scott Kennedy

Dear Sir or Madam:

     On November 6, 1990 Mr. Scott Kennedy was elected to the Santa
Cruz City Council.  The purpose of this letter is to elicit your
opinion in response to a conflict of interest question raised by
Mr. Kennedy.

     Mr. Kennedy is a half time employee of an organization known
as the Resource Center for Nonviolence (RCNV).  He has been
employed continuously with RCNV since 1982.  RCNV is one of several
programs operated by the Eschaton Foundation.  Eschaton is a non
profit, tax exempt, religious and educational foundation based in
Santa Cruz.  Eschaton is incorporated in the State of California
and has been granted 501(c) (3) status by the Internal Revenue
Service.  In general Eschaton sponsors a variety of programs
designed to promote peace and nonviolence.  RCNV is one of these
ongoing programs.

     For the most part Eschaton is an operating, rather than a
granting, foundation which means that its primary purpose is to
raise and receive funds in order to sustain its various programs.
However in 1989 Eschaton received a donation of approximately one
million dollars with the understanding that the funds would be
granted or loaned to various organizations by Eschaton.  An
advisory committee was formed by Eschaton to propose uses for these
funds.  Mr. Kennedy has served on this committee as a
noncompensated volunteer since its formation.  Prior to his
election to the City Council, the grant committee recommended that
$250,000.00  be loaned by Eschaton to the Community Housing
Corporation (CHC) to assist that entity in preparing for
the development of a low income housing project in downtown Santa
Cruz.  Pursuant to that recommendation, in May, 1990, Eschaton
loaned $250,000.00 to CHC for the acquisition and pre-development
financing of a parcel of property in downtown Santa Cruz.  The loan
is a short term note with a nine month repayment period.  The loan

FPPC
12/24/90
PAGE 2

is at seven percent simple interest and is secured by a deed of
trust on the property to be purchased. It should be noted that
RCNV, as a separate program, is responsible for its own
fundraising. These funds are segregated and is from these funds
that Mr. Kennedy is paid. The Eschaton grant money discussed above
is likewise segregated and is not used to subsidize RCNV either
directly or indirectly. Funds generated by Eschaton are not used
to compensate Mr. Kennedy.


CHC is a California non-profit corporation which is in the
business of developing low income and senior housing in Santa Cruz
County. Besides the project for which Eschaton lent CHC funds, CHC
is currently developing three other housing projects within the
City of Santa Cruz. Accordingly at this time CHC has four projects
pending for which it can expect to come to the City Council from
time to time for various permits and approvals.

     1. In light of the foregoing, and specifically in light of
the fact that Eschaton is currently a CHC creditor, we are
interested in determining whether, in your opinion, Mr. Kennedy has
a conflict of interest which would prevent him, as a councilmember,
from participating in decisions pertaining to the development of
the particular project for which Eschaton lent CHC funds?

     2. Each of CHC's four projects within the City of Santa Cruz
is separately funded. Is Mr. Kennedy nonetheless prohibited from
participating in Council decisions pertaining to the three CHC
projects which are not being funded by Eschaton or future non-
funded CHC projects?

     3. Finally, CHC is obligated to repay the Eschaton loan
within the near future. There is also a possibility that instead
of requiring repayment, Eschaton may transform the loan into a
grant. Under either of these scenarios, Eschaton would no longer
be a CHC creditor. Once Eschaton's relation to CHC as a creditor
has terminated will Mr. Kennedy have any conflicts of interest
which would prevent him in participating in future Council
decisions pertaining to CHC projects within the City of Santa Cruz?
We assume that the answer to this final question is no.

FPPC
12/24/90
PAGE 3

_____________

Thank you very much for your assistance with regard to the
foregoing. If you have any questions or need further
clarification, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,

JOHN G. BARISONE

JGB:law

cc: Scott Kennedy - Councilmember
Richard C. Wilson - City Manager
Mayor Yokoyama

C:\LETTERS\FAIRPOL.PRA
